label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out which of the two options is sarcastic. The options are (A) SCIENCE CAUSES VACCINES! and (B) EVOLUTION CAUSES VACCINES! Hmm. Let's break this down. First, I know that sarcasm often involves saying the opposite of what one means, usually with irony. So the key is to understand the context in which these statements are made. Let's start with the subject matter. Science and vaccines: Science is the systematic study of the structure and behavior of the physical and natural world through observation and experiment. Vaccines are a product of scientific research and development. So there's a direct link between science and vaccines. When people talk about vaccines, they typically cite scientific research as the reason they exist. Evolution and vaccines:
